channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How the 1994 World Cup kicked off America's love affair with soccer
item.description: Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?
item.content:encoded:
Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?

(Image credit: Lois Bernstein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Lack of U.S. response to hantavirus outbreak worries public health experts
item.description: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.content:encoded: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court rules against voters' referendum on redistricting
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.content:encoded: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Jahd Khalil

item.title: Why Trump's efforts to force Iran to concede to U.S. demands aren't working
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.content:encoded: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Organizers of a canceled human rights conference in Zambia say China intervened
item.description: Zambia was due to host Rights Con, the world's biggest digital rights gathering, this week -- but abruptly canceled the event. Organizers blame Chinese pressure for the decision.
item.content:encoded: Zambia was due to host Rights Con, the world's biggest digital rights gathering, this week -- but abruptly canceled the event. Organizers blame Chinese pressure for the decision.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Novelists Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer share notes on mothers in fiction
item.description: Ahead of Mother's Day, we brought together two novelists -- and friends -- Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer, to talk about what they love about reading and writing about mothers.
item.content:encoded: Ahead of Mother's Day, we brought together two novelists -- and friends -- Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer, to talk about what they love about reading and writing about mothers.
item.dc:creator: Justine Kenin

item.title: Meet the Tiny Desk Contest finalists: Noa Bar
item.description: A Tiny Desk Contest judge introduces us to finalists Noa Barr from Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded: A Tiny Desk Contest judge introduces us to finalists Noa Barr from Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Anamaria Artemisa Sayre

item.title: Tennessee Democrat speaks about his erased district
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Democratic Congressman Steve Cohen of Tennessee, who represents that state's 9th Congressional district, which could be eliminated based on a new redistricting map.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Democratic Congressman Steve Cohen of Tennessee, who represents that state's 9th Congressional district, which could be eliminated based on a new redistricting map.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hodges

item.title: What's behind Trump's pardons of people convicted of public corruption?
item.description: President Trump has granted pardons to officials who were convicted of public corruption while also dismantling a federal office responsible for investigating and prosecuting corruption allegations.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has granted pardons to officials who were convicted of public corruption while also dismantling a federal office responsible for investigating and prosecuting corruption allegations.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: After their shepherd dies, 'The Sheep Detectives' set out to solve the mystery
item.description: In The Sheep Detectives, a shepherd (Hugh Jackman) is murdered, and his flock -- yes, the woolly, four-footed critters -- resolves to solve the crime.
item.content:encoded: In The Sheep Detectives, a shepherd (Hugh Jackman) is murdered, and his flock -- yes, the woolly, four-footed critters -- resolves to solve the crime.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello
